---
name: wg-peers-metrics
description: VPN Peers/Online Grafana metric — how wireguard_peer_up is generated on LXC 113
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: edd2c70c-c44a-41c6-8988-e2692d2ba920
---

**`wireguard_peer_up` metric** (drives "VPN Online" / "24h Trend VPN Peers" panels on the `stemlabs-infra` Grafana dashboard at 10.33.1.46) is generated by `/usr/local/bin/wg-peers-metrics.sh` inside **LXC 113 (grafana)** — written to `/var/lib/prometheus/node-exporter/wireguard.prom`, exposed via node_exporter textfile collector (instance 10.33.1.46:9100), scraped by Prometheus 10.33.1.46:9090.

- Runner: systemd `wg-peers-metrics.service` (Type=simple, `while true; sleep 15` loop — NOT a timer). Edits to the script are picked up automatically on next 15s loop; no restart needed.
- **Detection method = rx-byte delta (changed 2026-06-02, replaces handshake-threshold).** The script SSHes CRS admin@10.33.1.45, reads each peer's **raw `rx` byte counter** (`[/interface/wireguard/peers/get $p rx]` returns raw bytes) + `comment` (role) per peer, keeps a state file `/var/lib/prometheus/node-exporter/.wg-rxstate` (`peer<TAB>rx<TAB>last_change_epoch`), and sets **up=1 if rx increased within the last WINDOW=40s**. rx = data received FROM the peer = true liveness (frozen the instant a peer disconnects/turns VPN off → down within ~40s, realtime). **Do NOT use tx** — CRS sends keepalives to every peer regardless, so tx increases even for disconnected peers (this was the trap). Handshake-age was abandoned: it can't be both realtime-on-disconnect AND non-flapping-when-idle (180s flapped when connected-idle, 600s stayed "stuck" ~10min after disconnect — both rejected by user who wanted true realtime).
- Caveat: relies on the peer SENDING (client-side PersistentKeepalive or real traffic). A peer connected but totally silent >40s reads as down — acceptable/correct for this use. Most clients keepalive every 25s so they stay up.
- Online count query (panels): `sum(wireguard_peer_up{interface="wg-clients",vpn_role=~"admin|user"})`.
- **Fixed 2026-06-02:** the original version determined up by **ICMP ping of the peer's 10.9.0.x allowed-address** — but the grafana LXC has no route into the wg-clients subnet, so every ping failed → all peers showed up=0 (panel stuck red at 0) even when connected. Rewrote to use CRS last-handshake (authoritative, no ping). To deploy: edit locally, base64 → `ssh root@<node-of-113> "echo <b64>|base64 -d>/tmp/wg.sh; pct push 113 /tmp/wg.sh /usr/local/bin/wg-peers-metrics.sh -perms 755"`. LXC 113 placement drifts (currently node4 10.33.1.23) — check `ls /etc/pve/nodes/*/lxc/113.conf` first.
- **Label collision (fixed 2026-06-02):** the textfile used label `role=`, but Prometheus job `node` applies target labels that set `role="lxc"` on the grafana LXC instance → without honor_labels, Prometheus renamed the textfile's `role` to `exported_role` and the panel query `role=~"admin|user"` matched only stale frozen-at-0 series. **Fix: textfile now uses `vpn_role=` (no collision), and both panels query `vpn_role=~"admin|user"`.** When editing labels, NEVER reuse `role` here.
- Panels "VPN Online" `sum(wireguard_peer_up{interface="wg-clients",vpn_role=~"admin|user"})` + "24h Trend — VPN Peers" (state-timeline, same filter) → **exclude `vpn_role="printer"` by design**. Dashboard uid `stemlabs-infra` (title "Stemlabs Dashboard"). Grafana admin: `admin`/`Yaimakmak888` (NOT thiraphat user).
- CRS-side ping (`/ping` from 10.33.1.45, which CAN route to 10.9.0.x) was evaluated as an idle-peer signal but clients drop ICMP on the tunnel → returns 0 even for connected peers, so NOT used. Handshake≤180s is the signal.
- **Threshold raised 180s→600s (2026-06-02):** 180s caused false-down flapping — a connected-but-idle peer (laptop network quiet / asleep) stops rehandshaking and crosses 180s → panel shows 0 even though "connected". 600s (10 min) absorbs idle gaps; cost = a real disconnect lingers as up to ~10 min. Edit `[[ $age -le 600 ]]` in `/usr/local/bin/wg-peers-metrics.sh` on LXC 113 to tune (service while-loop picks it up next 5s, no restart). Diagnosing "panel shows 0 / broke again": check CRS handshake (`/interface wireguard peers print detail`) vs the textfile — if textfile already matches CRS, it's not broken, just the threshold/idle flap.

- **Fixed 2026-06-03 "data comes & goes / No data / 24h trend gaps":** CRS SSH banner is jittery (reverse-DNS lookups → measured swing 0–28s). The script's `ConnectTimeout=5` aborted whenever banner >5s → `OUTPUT` empty → the while-loop rewrote `wireguard.prom` with **only the # HELP/# TYPE headers, no peer lines** → that scrape lost all 6 series → Grafana "No data"; next scrape (fast banner) it reappeared = intermittent gaps. CRS itself was fine (uptime/cpu normal), VPN never down — purely the collector. **Two-part fix in `/usr/local/bin/wg-peers-metrics.sh`:** (1) SSH opts now `-o ConnectTimeout=35 -o ServerAliveInterval=5 -o ServerAliveCountMax=6` to tolerate slow banner; (2) **guard `if [[ -z "${OUTPUT//[[:space:]]/}" ]]; then rm -f "$TMP"; exit 0; fi` right after the SSH read** → on a failed read it KEEPS the last good textfile instead of blanking it (no gaps even if a read fails). Verified: 6 peer-lines persist every 8s, no flicker. Deploy = base64 → `ssh root@<node-of-113> "echo <b64>|base64 -d>/tmp/wg.sh; pct push 113 /tmp/wg.sh /usr/local/bin/wg-peers-metrics.sh --perms 755"` (LXC 113 on node4 10.33.1.23 as of 2026-06-03; while-loop picks up edits next 5s, no restart). LXC 113 has NO curl/bc — use wget for Prometheus API at 127.0.0.1:9090.

Related: [[proxmox-cluster]] [[vpn-crs-rbac]]
